zh.lifeSummary: 當João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela於大約1160年出生在葡萄牙布拉加時，他的父親Fernão Peres de Guimarães62歲，母親Usco Godins de Lanhoso21歲。他和Maria Soares De Sousa至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1208年在葡萄牙布拉加吉馬奧斯享年49歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fernandes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Luis, Fernando, Jorge, Mario, Francisco, Orlando, Ana, Abilio, Americo.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Henrique, Ilidio, Paulo, Agostinho, Amadeu, Manoel, Aderito, Lourenco, Marcio, Serafim.

Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the personal name   Fernando  . This is one of the most common surnames in Portugal. It is also found in Goa and elsewhere on the west coast of India, having been taken there by Portuguese colonists.
Spanish: variant or altered form of Fernández (see Fernandez ).

variants.name: Fernando
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luis, Orlando, Aida, Carlos, Jesusa, Rodolfo, Ruben, Adela, Alfredo, Alicia.  Indian Rohan, Lalith, Nimal, Rajiv, Sanjeeva, Shanthi, Anil, Anusha, Dushan, Kapila, Lakshman, Mallika.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Fernando, a younger form of Ferdinando (see Ferdinand ). This surname is also found in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there, and as a result of the expulsion of the Jews from Spain and Portugal at the end of the 15th century, many of whom moved to Italy. It is also common in western India and especially in Sri Lanka, where it was taken by Portuguese colonists in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Riba
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Mendel, Yetta.  German Lothar, Erwin, Nikolaus.  Spanish Jose, Alfredo, Carlos, Claudio, Fidel, Manuel, Ramon.

Catalan: topographic name for someone who lived on the bank of a river or the shore of a lake, riba (from Latin ripa), or a habitational name from La Riba, a town in Alt Camp, Tarragona, or any other place called with this word.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): variant of   Ryba  ‘fish’.

variants.name: Ryba
meanings: Polish, Czech, Slovak, Sorbian, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name, nickname, or a Jewish artificial name from Polish, Czech, and Slovak ryba ‘fish’. As a metonymic occupational name it may have denoted a fisherman or a seller of fish (see also Ribar , Rybak , and Rybarczyk ). As a nickname it may have been bestowed on account of some fancied physical resemblance to a fish. The Sorbian surname is found mainly in Germanized forms, such as Riebe .

variants.name: Riva
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Digna, Mario, Ramon, Sergio, Alvaro, Angel, Armando, Benito, Cristina, Fernando, Guillermo.  Italian Angelo, Flavio, Agostino, Antonio, Italo, Sal, Santo, Sebastiano.  French Gaston, Jean-Paul.
Italian: topographic name for someone who lived by a wharf or landing at a port or seaport or on the bank of a river, from riva ‘river bank’ (from Latin ripa).
Catalan and Spanish: variant of   Riba  , which has the same origin and meaning as 1 above.

variants.name: Ribas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Eduardo, Jorge, Julio, Alberto, Armando, Carlos, Francisco, Mario, Mirta, Rafael.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo.
Catalan: Castilianized form of Ribes, a habitational name from any of the places called with Catalan ribes, plural of riba ‘bank’ (see Riba ).
Spanish and Galician: habitational name from any of the places called with the plural of riba ‘bank’ (see Riba ). Compare Rivas .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vizela
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Galician and Portuguese: habitational name from any of numerous places called Vilela, from a diminutive of vila ‘village, small town’ (see Vila ). Compare Villela .

variants.name: Vila
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Juan, Manuel, Jorge, Francisco, Pedro, Ramon, Eduardo, Efrain, Enrique.

Catalan: topographic name for someone who lived in a vila (see 3 below), which in Catalonia denoted a small town or village with certain privileges not common for villages, or a habitational name from any of the places called La Vila or Vila. This is one of the most common surnames in Catalonia.
Catalan (Vilà): variant of Vilar .

variants.name: Villela
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Felipe, Javier, Juan, Raymundo, Alfonso, Eduardo, Geraldo, Jose Cruz.
Spanish and Portuguese: altered form of Galician and Portuguese   Vilela  .

nameMeanings.name: João
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Portugues (João): from the personal name João, an equivalent of John .

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

en.lifeSummary: When João Fernandes de Riba de Vizela was born about 1160, in Braga, Portugal, his father, Fernão Peres de Guimarães, was 62 and his mother, Usco Godins de Lanhoso, was 21. He had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Maria Soares De Sousa. He died in 1208, in Guimarães, Braga, Portugal, at the age of 49.

Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Maria Soares De Sousa. Il est décédé en 1208, à Guimarães, Braga, Portugal, à l’âge de 49 ans.
